concept: Sage / Wise One / Elder of Wisdom
description: One whose insight comes from deep experience and reflection; a wise elder. 'Phewo' names wisdom earned through living, not only through study.
sound_symbolism: The fricative 'phe' opens on a quiet breath, and 'wo' rounds into the open back vowel. Both syllables are soft; the word contains no stops at all.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'phewo lo thunai thumela.' (The sage teaches the students.) Beyond 'thonua' (scholar)—wisdom rather than knowledge. The goal of 'thole' (practice) over a lifetime; compare 'wheo' (elder), aged but not necessarily wise.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sage as awakened—one who has realized through practice what others only study.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sage as elder—wisdom that comes from long life lived attentively.
